bio: Ned Presnall is a Licensed Clinical Social Worker and the owner and director of Plan Your Recovery. He uses cognitive behavioral therapy and empathic, interpersonal support to help clients understand the sources of their emotional distress and unwanted patterns of behavior. Building on this understanding, he helps clients draw on their strengths to plan and carry out meaningful change. For several years, Ned taught a graduate course on addiction at the Washington University Brown School of Social Work, and has served as a trainer for the Addiction Technology Transfer Center, the Missouri Department of Mental Health, and the Missouri Institute for Mental Health. He is president of the board of directors of MO Network and a university research collaborator. He advocates for the full integration of addiction treatment into mainstream healthcare.

Michelle's personal journey in recovery ignited a profound passion for supporting individuals in exploring their relationships with habits, self, and others. Employing a person-centered, strengths-based approach, she helps each client define their own recovery journey — guiding them from survival toward flourishing. She holds international certifications as a reciprocal substance use disorder counselor and a certified gambling disorder counselor, and is a certified facilitator of SMART Recovery and Seeking Safety. Michelle is also a registered yoga instructor and a certified Christian mental health coach. She currently serves as executive director of MoNetwork, a nonprofit recovery community center committed to harm reduction. She also leads the Thriving Beyond Recovery empowerment program for people in sustained recovery who are ready to build a fuller life.

fullName: Matt Silva, PsyDc, MBA, MA, LPC
bio: Matthew Silva has worked in the field of addiction for over 18 years. He has managed an acute medical stabilization unit and served as president and vice president at both inpatient and outpatient substance use programs. Matthew has served on the board of the Missouri Addiction Counselors Association and is graduate adjunct faculty at Maryville University. He uses person-centered approaches — particularly motivational interviewing — to help clients leverage their own wisdom and strengths in building the life they want.

Sean Sudekum received her master's in social work from Washington University in St. Louis. She specializes in the cognitive treatment of mental health problems, with particular focus on teens and young adults dealing with anxiety, depression, ADHD, and substance use. Sean uses psychodynamic therapy rooted in attachment theory to help clients understand the origins of their patterns, and cognitive therapy to help them change unhealthy ways of thinking and behaving.

Katherine Fezzi is a Licensed Master Social Worker specializing in addiction and co-occurring disorders. She has experience working with depression, anxiety, social anxiety, trauma, PTSD, disordered eating, attachment disorders, body dysmorphia, and codependent relationships. She uses a client-centered, cognitive behavioral approach to help clients achieve their goals. Katherine also facilitates a treatment group for individuals in early recovery. She holds a JD from Saint Louis University School of Law, and returned to school to earn her MSW in pursuit of her lifelong goal of practicing psychotherapy.

fullName: Angela Jones, MSN, APRN, PMHNP-BC, FNP-BC
bio: Angela Jones is a board-certified psychiatric nurse practitioner with dual certifications in psychiatric-mental health and family medicine nursing. She graduated with honors with her MSN from Maryville University and earned her post-master's PMHNP certificate from Southern Illinois University Edwardsville. Angela is a member of the American Association of Nurse Practitioners and serves on the board of Saint Louis Nurses in Advanced Practice. She is passionate about providing compassionate, comprehensive mental health care to adults. Angela has extensive experience treating addictive disorders and is certified to provide buprenorphine (Suboxone) treatment for opioid use disorder. Her collaborating psychiatrist is Dr. James Cho. Most appointments are virtual unless in-person is preferred.

John Gransbach is a doctorate-prepared Psychiatric-Mental Health Nurse Practitioner with over 10 years of experience in behavioral and mental health. He earned his Doctorate in Nursing Practice in 2023 from the University of Missouri-Columbia. John takes a holistic approach to mental health, integrating psychotherapy, psychopharmacology, behavior modification, nutrition, sleep, and psychoeducation to help patients and families achieve wellness. He has deep experience evaluating and treating dual-diagnosis patients — those with co-occurring psychiatric and addiction disorders — including medications for addiction treatment. His doctoral work focused on mindfulness-based cognitive therapy to help adolescents manage anxiety and reduce suicidal ideation. He received the St. Louis Magazine Excellence in Nursing Award in Behavioral Health and Psychiatry, and the Distinguished Young Alumni Award from Barnes-Jewish College Goldfarb School of Nursing for his work in suicide education and prevention.
